name: In-term AES Training Augmentation Keeps Real Essays as the Validation Boundary
statement: Yoo et al. provide lower-consequence in-term AES evidence where synthetic DREsS_CASE essays support training augmentation, but real classroom essays remain the validation and test evidence for rubric-based scoring.
population_or_context: Undergraduate EFL rubric-based automated essay scoring.
time_horizon: Full text retrieved and extracted on 2026-06-02.
quantitative_note: The staged artifact records 40,185 synthetic CASE samples and real DREsS_New validation/test use, but this finding emphasizes the use boundary rather than one metric.
caveats: CASE corruption is not the same as LLM simulation.
No direct operational consequence is assigned to the synthetic training examples.
evaluation_method: AES baselines evaluated with real validation/test data and rubric-score agreement metrics.
limitations: Bounded to EFL writing and the DREsS dataset design.

source_locator.quote_or_paraphrase_note: The finding paraphrases the separation between synthetic CASE training data and real validation/test essays.
